CAT10-046.headline: Analog Hobbies
CAT10-046.subtitle: How offline activities support mental health
CAT10-046.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans over its hands, absorbed in working something small and tactile, posture unhurried and quietly content, conveying the grounding pleasure of making things by hand.
CAT10-046.sceneLine: A cluster of soft amber rounded forms rests on a low surface beside the figure, each shaped a little differently in pale amber, suggesting simple offline pursuits that settle the mind.

CAT12-012.headline: Self-Stigma
CAT12-012.subtitle: When mental illness shame comes from within
CAT12-012.figureLine: A single seated clay figure curls slightly inward with one hand drawn toward its chest, posture turned away yet not collapsed, conveying shame that is quietly held within.
CAT12-012.sceneLine: A soft amber form drifts close around the figure like an inner shadow in soft light amber, gently loosening at its edges where pale amber light begins to break through, suggesting shame that can soften.

CAT12-013.headline: Why Men Avoid Help
CAT12-013.subtitle: How to change reluctance to seek support
CAT12-013.figureLine: A single standing clay figure pauses at a threshold with one hand half-raised, posture caught between hesitation and a small forward lean, conveying the moment before reaching out for help.
CAT12-013.sceneLine: A soft amber opening glows just ahead of the figure in soft light amber, its pale amber edges warm and unguarded, suggesting an invitation toward support that is safe to accept.

CAT19-005.headline: Emotional Literacy In Men
CAT19-005.subtitle: The language they were never taught
CAT19-005.figureLine: A single seated clay figure cups its open hands before it as if learning to hold something delicate, posture attentive and newly careful, conveying the slow learning of an inner language.
CAT19-005.sceneLine: Several small amber shapes assemble into a loose, legible pattern in the figure's open hands in soft light amber, each one distinct in pale amber, suggesting feelings finally given names.

CAT19-025.headline: Male Stoicism And Pain
CAT19-025.subtitle: Why ignoring physical pain is dangerous
CAT19-025.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds itself rigidly upright while one hand presses quietly to its side, posture braced and over-controlled, conveying discomfort silently endured.
CAT19-025.sceneLine: A taut amber line runs the length of the figure in amber accent, beginning to ease and soften into pale amber where the tension finally releases, suggesting pain that needs to be acknowledged rather than hidden.

CAT19-032.headline: Male Athletes
CAT19-032.subtitle: Mental health struggles in sport culture
CAT19-032.figureLine: A single clay figure sits apart from a posture of effort, shoulders lowered and hands resting on its knees, conveying the quiet weight carried beneath athletic performance.
CAT19-032.sceneLine: A tight amber ring of pressure surrounds the figure in amber accent, opening at one side into softer pale amber space, suggesting room to be human inside a demanding sport culture.

CAT19-040.headline: Unemployment And Identity
CAT19-040.subtitle: When work defines self-worth
CAT19-040.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands without a task before it, hands hanging open and uncertain, posture searching for footing, conveying self-worth shaken when work falls away.
CAT19-040.sceneLine: A scattering of soft amber forms lies loose around the figure's feet in soft light amber, with steadier pale amber ground beginning to gather underfoot, suggesting worth that exists beyond a job.

CAT19-045.headline: Talking Isn't Weakness
CAT19-045.subtitle: The myth that men sharing feelings is weak
CAT19-045.figureLine: A single standing clay figure opens its chest and lifts its head with both hands turned outward, posture unguarded and quietly strong, conveying the real courage of speaking openly.
CAT19-045.sceneLine: A solid amber form steadies beneath the figure in amber accent while a softer pale amber thread rises freely from its open hands, suggesting that honesty and strength belong together.

CAT19-053.headline: Men And Meditation
CAT19-053.subtitle: Overcoming the stigma that mindfulness is soft
CAT19-053.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests in a grounded, settled stillness with hands loose on its knees, posture solid rather than fragile, conveying that quiet strength is found in stillness.
CAT19-053.sceneLine: A broad amber pool of calm spreads steadily outward around the seated figure in soft light amber, its pale amber surface unbroken and firm, suggesting mindfulness as durable rather than soft.

CAT19-058.headline: Journaling For Men
CAT19-058.subtitle: Writing as an emotional processing tool
CAT19-058.figureLine: A single seated clay figure bends over a flat surface with one hand moving steadily across it, posture private and absorbed, conveying the quiet release of putting feeling into form.
CAT19-058.sceneLine: A tangle of soft amber strands beside the figure draws out into a single ordered amber line in soft light amber, smoothing into pale amber, suggesting thoughts untangled through writing.

CAT19-063.headline: Anger As A Gateway
CAT19-063.subtitle: The emotion that masks deeper feeling in men
CAT19-063.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds a tense, outward-pushing stance with one fist drawn back, posture sharp on the surface yet softening at the shoulders, conveying anger that guards something tender underneath.
CAT19-063.sceneLine: A hard amber outer shell surrounds the figure in amber accent, parting to reveal a warmer pale amber core within, suggesting anger as the surface of a deeper feeling.

CAT19-064.headline: The Provider Mandate
CAT19-064.subtitle: How financial identity harms men's mental health
CAT19-064.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands square beneath a weight pressing on its shoulders, arms taking the load, posture dutiful and strained, conveying identity bound too tightly to providing.
CAT19-064.sceneLine: A heavy amber mass rests across the figure's shoulders in amber accent, beginning to lighten into pale amber as it lifts, suggesting a burden of expectation that can be set down.

CAT19-065.headline: Creative Expression
CAT19-065.subtitle: How making things helps men process emotion
CAT19-065.figureLine: A single seated clay figure shapes something freely with both hands, posture loose, open, and absorbed, conveying emotion finding its way out through making.
CAT19-065.sceneLine: A flowing amber form streams from the figure's hands and takes shape in the air in soft light amber, settling into expressive pale amber curves, suggesting feeling given form through creativity.

CAT19-066.headline: When Humor Heals
CAT19-066.subtitle: And when it masks pain in men
CAT19-066.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds a light, buoyant stance while one hand rests quietly over its chest, posture playful above and tender beneath, conveying humor that both heals and conceals.
CAT19-066.sceneLine: A bright amber form lifts lightly in front of the figure in soft light amber while a softer pale amber shape rests half-hidden behind it, suggesting laughter that can either lighten or cover real pain.
